title: Podman in atomic linux
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST?**:
 /kind feature

**Description**

Currently podman is a bit tricky to install with atomic linux because of all the build dependencies and because `make install` does a few other things on your system its not appropriate to just build the artifact in a container and use it. What would be the suggested way to get podman running in atomic without having to install all the dependencies?

comments.body: Is something similar to `make dbuild` in the Makefile (builds Podman in a Docker container) sufficient?

We are working to get it into Atomic Host.  We just need to get the vendored in podman build and podman commit that @baude is working on. so it does not require buildah and we should be able to get it in.
cool, thank you, really looking forward to using it.
I've seen it on F28 AH as well already.
@baude - maybe it is time for me to rebase then! Definitely had some issues trying to figure out the best way to do it on 27 AH without installing all the dependencies

If you want to build stuff from source and run on AH, see https://fedorapeople.org/~walters/2018.01-devconf-desktopcontainers/#/7

I do this a lot.  Practically speaking the simplest is to probably `rpm-ostree install podman` on F27AH, then build from source and then copy the built `podman` binary over to `/usr/bin/podman` (with `rpm-ostree usroverlay` active).  That's *generally* going to work.

But of course it won't cover other things; if you want more a full e2e flow it's sadly going to involve mashing git builds into RPMs.
@cgwalters thanks very much for this, that totally solved my issue.
